title: Code-enforced non-emergency refusal layer
description: Close the production-grade guardrail gap: the {kind:'refuse'} verdict already exists in GuardrailVerdict (src/medical/types.ts:14) and the 'refuse' AuditEvent exists (src/medical/types.ts:69), but MedicalGuardrails.evaluate (src/medical/guardrails.ts:80-98) never emits refuse — prescription/dosing/treatment-plan prompts fall through to {kind:'allow'} and are only refused prompt-only by the LLM. Add a deterministic, pure, synchronous RefusalDetector (sibling to RedFlagDetector, src/medical/red-flag.ts) that classifies a prompt into prescription | specific-dosing | individualized-treatment-plan | none. Wire MedicalGuardrails.evaluate to run RedFlagDetector FIRST (short-circuit wins), then RefusalDetector, returning {kind:'refuse', rule, reason} with FIXED never-model-generated reason text (mirror the canned escalation strings at guardrails.ts:30-39). Add a refuse-dispatch branch in MedicalSopEngine.run mirroring the existing consent-refuse path (engine.ts:217-248): return a MedicalAnswer carrying the canned reason with shortCircuit:true, append an audit entry event:'refuse' with ruleId/rulesetVersion/patternsetVersion (IDs/enums only), and reach ZERO downstream numerics/retrieval/LLM. GuardrailContext stays empty (ADR-3). This sprint is self-contained, has no network, and does not touch WHOOP.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build) and zero typecheck errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
The full test suite passes with zero failures including new collocated src/medical/refusal*.test.ts (npm run test); no regression in the pre-existing suite.

RefusalDetector.detect(prompt) returns category 'prescription' for prescription requests, 'specific-dosing' for specific dose requests, 'individualized-treatment-plan' for personalized treatment/care-plan requests, and 'none' otherwise; it is pure and synchronous (no async/fs/network/LLM) and exposes a patternsetVersion. A unit test covers each category plus benign 'none' cases.

MedicalGuardrails.evaluate returns {kind:'refuse', rule, reason} for each refuse category with FIXED reason text (asserted byte-equal to a constant, proving it is not model-generated), and still returns {kind:'allow'} for benign prompts.

When a prompt matches BOTH a red-flag and a refuse pattern, evaluate returns the red-flag {kind:'short-circuit'} (emergency precedence), not refuse. A unit test constructs such a prompt and asserts short-circuit wins.

MedicalSopEngine.run on a refuse-category prompt returns a MedicalAnswer with body===the canned reason, shortCircuit:true, abstained:false, citations:[], and appends exactly one audit entry with event:'refuse' carrying ruleId/rulesetVersion/patternsetVersion and NO prompt text or health values.

On a refuse-category prompt, an injected spy LLMClient is invoked 0 times and NumericsQueryLayer / FactStore / LiteratureRetriever are never reached (0 LLM, 0 egress) — asserted with a spy that fails if called, mirroring the red-flag spy assertion at engine.ts:53-56.

No network/LLM import is added under src/medical for this sprint and the scoped ESLint medical boundary still passes (npm run lint); RefusalDetector and the guardrails change introduce no @anthropic-ai/sdk, openai, or node:net/http imports.

nonGoals: Do not add WHOOP, any egress axis, or any network code (Sprints 2-3).
Do not add fields to GuardrailContext — it stays the empty placeholder (ADR-3).
Do not use an LLM or in-line model filter for refusal classification — deterministic patterns only (research REFUTED the LLM policy filter).
Do not change RedFlagDetector behavior or the existing short-circuit/consent/allow paths beyond inserting the refuse branch.
Do not alter the MedicalSopEngine.run signature or the zero-arg constructor.
stopConditions: A refuse-category prompt yields a code-enforced refusal + 'refuse' audit entry with 0 LLM calls, red-flag still wins over refuse, benign prompts still allow, and the build/typecheck/test/lint gates are green with no regression
definitionOfDone: src/medical/refusal.ts exports a pure synchronous RefusalDetector { detect(prompt): RefusalMatch; readonly patternsetVersion } with category union prescription|specific-dosing|individualized-treatment-plan|none and fixed canned reason strings. src/medical/guardrails.ts evaluate() runs RedFlagDetector then RefusalDetector and returns {kind:'refuse', rule, reason} on a match. src/medical/engine.ts adds a refuse-dispatch branch after the short-circuit branch, mirroring the consent-refuse path, returning the canned MedicalAnswer and appending an event:'refuse' audit entry (IDs/enums only). GuardrailContext unchanged. Collocated tests cover detection, fixed-text, red-flag precedence, engine dispatch + audit, and the spy-LLM-never-called assertion. Build, typecheck, test, and lint pass with no regression.
assumptions: RefusalDetector mirrors RedFlagDetector's shape (src/medical/red-flag.ts:195-212): a pure detect(prompt) returning { category, ruleId? } plus a versioned patternsetVersion, no constructor dependencies.
MedicalGuardrails.evaluate keeps its signature evaluate(prompt, ctx): GuardrailVerdict (src/medical/types.ts:25-28); the empty-prompt throw at guardrails.ts:81-83 is retained.

The refuse audit entry uses the existing AuditEntry shape (src/medical/types.ts:77-87) with event:'refuse'; the engine already has an AuditLog instance and a now/runId injection seam (engine.ts opts).
The canned refuse reason text is a decline + see-a-licensed-clinician message (distinct from the 911/988 emergency escalations), fixed per category and never passed through an LLM.
outOfScope: Any change to existing engine/guardrail public behavior beyond the refuse branch
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST: src/medical/red-flag.ts (full — copy its pure-detector structure, versioned patternset, and category-switch idiom), src/medical/guardrails.ts:1-104 (where evaluate lives and where the placeholder comment at :94-95 says refusals land), src/medical/engine.ts:191-289 (gate ordering; the consent-refuse path at :217-248 is the exact template for the refuse branch; the red-flag short-circuit branch at :255-289 and the spy assertion at :53-56), src/medical/types.ts (GuardrailVerdict :11-14, AuditEvent :64-71, AuditEntry :77-87, GuardrailContext :18-22).

New file src/medical/refusal.ts: export type RefusalCategory = 'prescription'|'specific-dosing'|'individualized-treatment-plan'|'none'; export const REFUSAL_PATTERNSET_VERSION = 'refusal-2026.06.17'; export class RefusalDetector { detect(prompt: string): RefusalMatch; get patternsetVersion() }. Use conservative case-insensitive phrase/keyword matching (e.g. 'prescribe', 'what dose', 'how many mg', 'should I take ... mg', 'treatment plan for me', 'what should I do to treat my'). Keep the patternset small, reviewable, and versioned — accept false-negatives (fall through to 'none'), NEVER false-positive into medical advice. Define fixed canned reason strings per category as module constants (mirror ESCALATION_911/988 at guardrails.ts:30-39).

guardrails.ts: instantiate a readonly RefusalDetector alongside the existing RedFlagDetector; in evaluate(), after the red-flag match check (which returns short-circuit), add: const r = this.refusal.detect(prompt); if (r.category !== 'none') return { kind:'refuse', rule: r.ruleId ?? r.category, reason: REASON[r.category] }; then the existing return { kind:'allow' }. Expose the refusal patternsetVersion if the engine needs it for the audit (add a getter like the existing patternsetVersion at guardrails.ts:101-103).

engine.ts: after the short-circuit dispatch branch, add `if (verdict.kind === 'refuse') { await auditLog.append({ tIso: now, event:'refuse', ruleId: verdict.rule, rulesetVersion: guardrails.rulesetVersion, patternsetVersion: <refusal patternset> }); return <MedicalAnswer {body: verdict.reason, abstained:false, citations:[], disclaimerFooter: disclaimer.footer(), shortCircuit:true}> wrapped into a PipelineResult exactly as the consent-refuse path does }. Confirm zero calls to numerics/factstore/retriever/llm on this path.

Tests: refusal.test.ts (each category + 'none'); guardrails.test.ts additions (refuse verdict + fixed text + red-flag precedence + allow); engine.test.ts additions (refuse MedicalAnswer shape + single 'refuse' audit entry with no prompt text + spy LLMClient never invoked using the existing spy pattern). Pure/no-network; follow ESM/.js import extensions and unicode section headers.
evaluatorNotes:
sc-1-3: call RefusalDetector.detect on representative prompts for each category and on benign questions; confirm purity (no async signature, no imports of fs/net/providers in refusal.ts via grep).
sc-1-4: assert the returned reason is byte-equal to the exported constant (proves fixed/not model-generated); assert allow on a benign prompt.
sc-1-5: craft a prompt containing both an emergency red-flag trigger and a refuse trigger; assert evaluate returns kind==='short-circuit' (red-flag wins).
sc-1-6/sc-1-7: drive MedicalSopEngine.run with a refuse-category prompt and a spy LLMClient that throws if called; assert MedicalAnswer.body===canned reason, shortCircuit===true, abstained===false, citations===[]; read the audit jsonl and assert exactly one event:'refuse' entry containing ruleId/rulesetVersion/patternsetVersion and NO prompt substring or health value; assert the spy recorded 0 calls.
sc-1-8: npm run lint clean; grep src/medical/refusal.ts and the guardrails diff for '@anthropic-ai/sdk', 'openai', 'node:net', 'node:http', 'fetch' — expect none.
Build + typecheck clean; run the FULL suite to confirm no regression in the existing medical and non-medical tests.
